_comment: AI Capex Reality Engine (Engine 17) universe taxonomy. Curated map of the AI-infrastructure trade across 13 categories plus second-order linkage edges. Edits here flow straight into the engine — no code change needed. Tickers are liquid US listings; keep lists tight (the LLM extractor + scorer run per ticker).

semis.name: Semiconductors / AI Accelerators
semis.blurb: GPU/accelerator, foundry, and chip-equipment names that monetize AI compute demand directly.

memory.name: Memory (DRAM / HBM / NAND)
memory.blurb: High-bandwidth memory and storage that gates accelerator shipments; tight supply is a real-capex tell.

networking.name: Networking / Optical / Fiber
networking.blurb: Switching, optical transceivers, and fiber that scale with cluster buildouts.

cloud_providers.name: Cloud / Hyperscalers
cloud_providers.blurb: Hyperscaler capex guidance and cloud backlog are the upstream driver for the whole chain.

data_center_reits.name: Data-Center REITs
data_center_reits.blurb: Colo/wholesale data-center landlords whose leasing + development pipeline reflects real buildout.

power_infrastructure.name: Power Generation / IPPs
power_infrastructure.blurb: Independent power producers and gas/nuclear names benefiting from data-center load growth.

utilities.name: Regulated Utilities
utilities.blurb: Utilities with data-center interconnection demand and rate-base growth from load additions.

electrical_equipment.name: Electrical Equipment / Transformers
electrical_equipment.blurb: Transformers, switchgear, grid gear, and power management — lead times here are a hard-evidence tell.

cooling.name: Cooling / Thermal Management
cooling.blurb: Liquid cooling, HVAC, and thermal vendors scaling with rack power density.

construction.name: Construction / Engineering
construction.blurb: E&C and equipment-rental names building the physical data centers and substations.

industrial_automation.name: Industrial Automation
industrial_automation.blurb: Automation and electrification suppliers with AI-factory and grid exposure.

cybersecurity.name: Cybersecurity
cybersecurity.blurb: Security names with genuine AI-product monetization vs AI marketing language.

ai_software.name: AI Software / Applications
ai_software.blurb: Software names — separate real AI revenue/monetization from 'AI' positioning with no numbers behind it.

second_order_edges._comment: When a DRIVER category shows real, corroborated capex, these beneficiary categories are the second-order winners. Weight scales how strongly the driver's reality propagates.

second_order_losers._comment: Categories that lose when AI-capex is over-earning / mean-reverts, or that are crowded out (e.g. power scarcity, capital reallocation). Used to flag 2nd-order loser candidates when driver evidence turns to delay/overhype.
